title: Blindspots Register — what the world-map cannot currently see
purpose: Honest meta-layer required by PROTOCOL: log the extra-context search trail and where the map is blind (source-tier limits, source disagreements, modeled-not-measured figures, ungrounded gaps, process risks) so confidence is never read without its caveat. Mirrors notes/blindspots.jsonl (kind=blindspot); this file is the structured, groupable register.

severity_scale.m: could mislead a claim or a non-critical decision
severity_scale.h: threatens a core decision or a P0/P1 directive if wrong

blindspots.blindspot: Synergy Q3 2025 (63% big-three; 29/20/13) vs CRN Q4 2025 (~68%; ~28/21/15) disagree — likely different metric/quarter, unreconciled.
queries_run: cloud infrastructure market share Q4 2025 AWS Azure Google Synergy Research percent
blindspots.surfaced: Anchored map on Synergy Q3 (scraped); noted CRN Q4 as newer/possibly-different-metric.
blindspots.caveat: One analyst firm, one quarter; metric definitions not reconciled; no Canalys/IDC triangulation.
blindspots.verify_to_close: Pull Canalys + IDC quarterly tracker, reconcile CIS vs total-enterprise-spend definitions, fix the map on one quarter+metric.

blindspots.blindspot: Egress/storage per-GB rates ($0.09 AWS, $0.0085 OCI, hot $0.018-0.023) from one FinOps vendor blog, not official pricing pages.
queries_run: AWS Azure Google Cloud data egress pricing per GB 2025 comparison USD
blindspots.surfaced: Corroborated across search snippets (CloudBolt, emma.ms) but not primary-verified.
blindspots.caveat: Tertiary source; regional/tiered exceptions not captured.
blindspots.verify_to_close: Scrape AWS, OCI, Azure, GCP official egress + object-storage pricing pages directly.

blindspots.blindspot: Cloud LLM per-token rates from a third-party aggregator (April 2026 snapshot), not provider pages; rates drift monthly.
queries_run: LLM API pricing per million tokens 2026 GPT Claude Gemini input output cost comparison
blindspots.surfaced: Coherent rate card; flagged hr:m for drift.
blindspots.caveat: Secondary aggregator; no fact-check vs OpenAI/Anthropic/Google pricing pages; likely stale within weeks.
blindspots.verify_to_close: Scrape the three providers' pricing pages; wire the router cost model to a live-rate feed instead of a snapshot.

blindspots.blindspot: Inference crossover ($7.15/1M local vs $6.90/1M OpenAI) is a TCO MODEL on 50M-tok/day datacenter hardware, not measured, not consumer-device.
queries_run: on-device LLM inference cost vs cloud API price per million tokens 2025 Apple Neural Engine local
blindspots.surfaced: Directional crossover + sensitivities (EU power 2x; refresh +$130-200k); flagged op:1 + hr:m.
blindspots.caveat: Modeled, single source, datacenter-scale — wrong granularity for our consumer-device router.
blindspots.verify_to_close: Replace with a measured/consumer-device TCO once the on-device curve is grounded.

blindspots.blindspot: No grounded data on real on-device inference throughput/cost for a baseline consumer device (Apple Neural Engine / M-series / NPU) at our token mix.
blindspots.surfaced: Deferred this pass; sitepoint covers datacenter GPUs only.
blindspots.caveat: Highest-leverage unknown for the hybrid-router directive; router cost model is theoretical until closed.
blindspots.verify_to_close: Benchmark Core ML / MLX / Ollama on M-series + a mid Android NPU for our token profile; emit measured $/token.

blindspots.blindspot: Apple Private Cloud Compute + Apple/Google on-device foundation-model third-party access terms ungrounded — premise of the dc-cloud-3 inference-gate claim.
blindspots.surfaced: Flagged as gap wm-cl-g-apple-pcc; distinctive claim dc-cloud-3 kept at low confidence 0.48.
blindspots.caveat: Distinctive claim rests on an unverified premise.
blindspots.verify_to_close: Scrape Apple Foundation Models framework + Private Cloud Compute terms and Android AICore/Gemini Nano third-party terms.

blindspots.blindspot: '10x/yr inference cost decline' is community/Reddit-sourced (tertiary), directional only.
blindspots.caveat: Primary (a16z/blog) not pulled; ordinal hint only.
blindspots.verify_to_close: Pull the primary cost-decline analysis if this becomes load-bearing.

blindspots.blindspot: Most facts rest on one source per claim; few carry 2+ independent primary sources — wide but thinly triangulated map; correlated source error would pass uncaught.
blindspots.surfaced: Observed reconciling notes/*; distribution + regulation best-grounded, cloud + SmartScreen-tertiary weakest.
blindspots.caveat: PROTOCOL principle 7 (base rates/triangulation) under-applied at the sweep tier; dialectic (T9) meant to catch it but only run on a subset.
blindspots.verify_to_close: Run T9 dialectic on all op:1 claims; require a 2nd independent source for any claim feeding a P0 directive.

blindspots.blindspot: HANDOFF.md drifted out of sync (claimed 'NOT STARTED' while T1-T7 + partial T8-T13 existed). Docs and data have no freshness/owner link.
blindspots.surfaced: Reconciled manually from git + files; HANDOFF refreshed this pass.
blindspots.caveat: Future instance could trust a stale cold-start doc over the actual artifacts.
threatens: onboarding of the next processor instance
blindspots.verify_to_close: Add a 'last-reconciled' stamp to HANDOFF and/or generate the status section from notes/claims automatically.

blindspots.blindspot: Whether mainstream OS AI features are Annex III high-risk under AI Act Art.6 is genuinely unsettled in law (pending delegated acts) — dc-reg-2 rests on an open question.
blindspots.surfaced: Primary law text grounded; classification is forward-looking; flagged gap.
blindspots.caveat: Honest unresolved — cannot be closed by scraping, only by Commission action over time.
blindspots.verify_to_close: Track Commission delegated acts + harmonised guidance on high-risk classification through 2026.
